content: Section capacity systems display remaining-seats count to students (e.g. '28/30' or 'FULL/Closed') and block enrollment attempts once capacity is reached. Reserve capacity (seats held for a subgroup) is common in real SIS but is an advanced feature; the core universal pattern is a hard cap with a live count and a closed/full state once reached. Concurrency (two students grabbing the last seat simultaneously) is a known race condition risk; standard mitigation is a DB-level check-and-increment inside a transaction (or a unique/capacity constraint) rather than only a read-then-write check in application code.
